paper_title: Geophysical and Sediment Data Collection and Analysis on the Bug Peninsula

methods.description: Process for collecting geophysical data using ground-penetrating radar (GPR) to determine bedding structures within the upper sediment layers of the Bug peninsula.

steps.description: Conducted GPR surveys at the central and southern peninsula using a SIR-10 and SIR-20 set from GSSI with a 100 MHz antenna.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct GPR surveys with 100 MHz antenna

steps.description: Recorded reflectors for two-way-travel times of up to 200 ns and related them to depth using a constant velocity of 0.07 m/ns.
steps.searchable_summary: process GPR data to relate reflectors to depth

steps.description: Recorded additional GPR profiles along two previous tracks with different antenna frequencies (200, 100, and 50 MHz) using a RAMAC-GPR set.
steps.searchable_summary: record additional GPR profiles with RAMAC-GPR set

methods.description: Procedure for collecting sediment cores and analyzing their lithological units to characterize the sediment composition and depositional environments.

steps.description: Identified 16 core sites based on GPR measurements where reflectors were clearly visible.
steps.searchable_summary: identify core sites based on GPR data

steps.description: Collected sediment cores using a hydraulic powered drill and extraction tool with different diameters of half-open testing probes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores with hydraulic drill

steps.description: Classified cores into lithological units characterized by grain size, calcium carbonate, and organic content.
Measured granulometric composition of samples by dry sieving and optical laser sizer device.
Determined organic content as loss on ignition at 550 °C and carbonate content qualitatively.
Analyzed palaeo-environments by determining faunal remnants from samples.
